title: Noetfield / SourceA Cloud Factory — Commercial Product Category Registry
status_patch: PRODUCT_CATEGORY_LOCK_STATUS_PATCH_v1 applied: CAT-03/CAT-04/CAT-06 statuses corrected from verified receipts. See receipts/PRODUCT_CATEGORY_LOCK_STATUS_PATCH_v1.json. PRECONDITION #1 DONE (PRODUCT_CATEGORY_LOCK_SYNC_v1): 4 CAT-03/04 proving receipts recovered byte-exact from git 2f2f38b (branch cursor/machine-autonomy-loops-v1) and written to canonical receipts/p0pgr/ after the archived clone (all of ~/Projects) was deleted; 2 cloud cycle receipts (P0PGR-CYCLE-20260709T000048Z/000050Z) were never committed and are unrecoverable (evidence embedded in the R2 PASS receipt). Root-cause reverse-drift reconciliation remains open as precondition #3.

locked_in.SG: sina-governance-SSOT (branch cursor/language-layer-v1, HEAD 9f6773a) — draft, uncommitted, pending founder ratification

north_star: Noetfield / SourceA Cloud Factory Platform lets Sina or clients issue orders that are converted by P0-PGR into governed prompt packets, routed through cloud factory lines, executed in isolated sandboxes/worktrees, verified with receipts, and surfaced through Studio IDE / client workspace.
purpose: Lock the identity of the commercial product categories so execution resumes only against this registry. This is a CATEGORY-MAP lock, not a build claim: it fixes what each category IS, how it differs, and the receipt that gates its activation. It does not assert that any category is finished, live, or sellable.
governing_constraints: Noetfield's public GTM is narrowly locked to THREE contract SKUs only (Trust Brief, Copilot Governance Pack, Bank Pilot) per Noetfield/OFFERINGS_LOCKED.md. The 10 categories below are PLATFORM / INTERNAL product categories (the factory that builds and governs), NOT new public SKUs.

Noetfield constitution: 'Noetfield never touches value — only policy, compliance logic, and traceability.' Any payment/onboarding framing (esp. TrustField) is a governance/decision layer, never money movement, custody, settlement, or routing.
Product/canon boundary (P10 product-vs-canon-boundary.md): product docs describe WHAT is sold; they cross-reference canon (HOW the system runs) but never compete with it.
This lock creates NO public claim, buys NO domain, merges NO branch, deploys NOTHING, and defines NO new category. Workers may not add categories; execution resumes only against this registry.

evidence_basis.method: 20-agent evidence + adversarial-verify workflow (run wf_0e4cdcf8-c34): one read-only evidence gatherer per category, then one downgrade-biased verifier per category re-checking each build-status claim against its cited proof assets.
evidence_basis.note: Every proof-asset path cited below was confirmed to exist. Build statuses were adversarially re-checked against a known 'fake-green / inflated-capability' failure mode in this ecosystem (SourceA incident reports 048/049). Honesty caveats per category record what is NOT yet true.
evidence_basis.environment_limits: Read-only, offline pass: no GitHub Actions API, no live HTTP fetches, no gh auth. 'live/running' claims that depend on CI run history or production HTTP are receipt-attested, not re-observed this pass — flagged per category.

changed: CAT-04 (TrustField loops built PR#10, RECONCILED_PASS, still not VERIFIED)
CAT-06 (kept partial-scaffold; NOT advanced by P0-PGR R2 proof)
status_patch_v1.verification: Each corrected status was confirmed against the actual receipt before patching (not accepted on assertion).
status_patch_v1.reverse_drift_finding: CRITICAL: the CAT-03 R2 PASS receipt (P0PGR_R2_CLOUD_MANUAL_PASS_v1-20260709T001540Z.json, sha256 8f43f2e8...) and the CAT-04 receipts (trustfield-cloud-loops-built / P0PGR_DUAL_SCAFFOLD_RECONCILIATION RECONCILED_PASS / TRUSTFIELD_CLOUD_LOOPS_ACTIVATION) exist ONLY in ~/Projects/sina-governance-ssot.MIGRATED-2026-07-08 (an archived clone), NOT in canonical ~/Desktop/Noetfield-Systems/sina-governance-SSOT. The two cloud cycle receipts the R2 PASS depends on (P0PGR-CYCLE-20260709T000048Z/000050Z) are also absent from canonical. RESOLUTION (PRODUCT_CATEGORY_LOCK_SYNC_v1): the archived clone was subsequently DELETED; the 4 proving receipts were recovered byte-exact from git commit 2f2f38b (hashes re-verified against the preserved anchors) and written to canonical receipts/p0pgr/. The 2 cycle receipts were never committed and remain unrecoverable (their per-run evidence is embedded in the R2 PASS receipt's per_run_checks). Root-cause reconciliation (where new P0-PGR/TrustField work lands now that ~/Projects is gone) remains open as precondition #3.

build_status_vocabulary.live-running: Real executions happen and emit receipts; the capability runs, not just parses.
build_status_vocabulary.built-not-activated: Real runnable artifacts exist (code/schemas/workflows/receipts) but the packaged product / external activation does not exist yet.
build_status_vocabulary.partial-scaffold: Some runnable pieces exist but the end-to-end capability is not wired; core spec or gate still 'to be built'.
build_status_vocabulary.concept-only: Named and reasoned about; no product artifact of this category exists.

status_ladder.P2: BUILT-SCAFFOLD — runnable pieces, not wired end-to-end
status_ladder.P3: BUILT-NOT-ACTIVATED — wired/runnable internally, no external/commercial activation
status_ladder.P4: LIVE-RUNNING (INTERNAL) — real executions emit receipts inside the factory
status_ladder.P5: PROOF-LANE VALIDATED — external/vertical proof with outcome receipts
status_ladder.P6: COMMERCIAL-ACTIVATION-GATED — packaged; founder + receipt gate to sell
status_ladder.mechanism: Grounded in the real proof loop: sandbox proposes -> independent verifier checks -> promotion gate -> only PASS reaches live; STEP-10-class actions are founder-gated (PHASE_LOOP_BUILD_PLAN_v0.1.md).

domain_posture_vocabulary.posture_now: For ALL categories the current posture is 'umbrella-internal': no standalone domain, no public SKU, no public claim — this enforces the founder lock rules.
domain_posture_vocabulary.target: umbrella = intended to live under noetfield.com / sourcea.app; subdomain = own subdomain once proven; standalone-later = own commercial domain/SKU only after its activation receipt exists.

lock_rules: Do not let workers define new categories.
Lock categories first; execution resumes only against this registry.

categories.product_name_status: PROPOSED — the names MemoryGate / DecisionGraph / Governed Agent Memory appear in ZERO files across SG, SourceA, and Noetfield; this is a commercial framing over real internal governance tooling.
categories.buyer: Now: the founder's own Noetfield/SourceA multi-agent ecosystem (internal). Later: operators of autonomous / multi-agent systems burned by agents acting on stale, superseded, or unauthorized context who need provenance + freshness + receipts over agent memory.
categories.pain_solved: Agent memory (vector/Graphiti/Zep/Mem0) has no authority, provenance, freshness, ROI, or execution gate — you cannot prove why an agent 'remembered' something, whether the memory was still live law, or whether the agent was allowed to act on it.
categories.noetfield_differentiation: A governance/gate layer OVER memory rather than a retrieval store: authority-scoped read surfaces (ACTIVE/SUPERSEDED/RETIRED), a staleness engine that BLOCKS on stale law, sha256 provenance chains with independent verification, per-stage decision receipts, and execution/promotion gates. Memory can block an action, not just recall text.

domain_strategy.note: No public surface today; earn standalone status only after the three lanes unify into one governed-memory product with an ROI lane and a non-founder consumer.

categories.build_status_rationale: The freshness, provenance, and decision-receipt lanes are genuinely built and executing internally, but they are three separate scripts/registries — there is no unified 'governed agent memory' interface, no ROI attribution tied to memory, no external consumer, and no product/SKU/domain. Built, not a stub; not activated as a product.
categories.next_build_phase: Unify the three lanes behind one authority-scoped memory interface, add the missing ROI-attribution lane (tie a prevented bad deploy/spend to a memory gate), and produce ONE composite end-to-end receipt where a single agent action is allowed/blocked by combined authority+freshness+provenance checks. Then one non-founder design-partner before any external claim.
categories.not_now_boundary: No public claim; no Graphiti/Zep/Mem0/vector-memory competitor positioning (Graphiti was rejected internally, not beaten as a product); no 'ROI' claim until an attribution lane exists; no MemoryGate/DecisionGraph brand, domain, or SKU; no claim of external users.
categories.receipt_required_to_activate: MEMORYGATE_COMPOSITE_GATE_RECEIPT — one composite receipt in which a single agent action was gated by combined authority-scope + freshness + sha256 provenance, PLUS one non-founder consumer, PLUS one ROI line attributing a specific prevented bad deploy/spend to the memory gate.
honesty_caveats: Product names exist nowhere in code.
Seed drift: data/brain_domain_registry_v1.json does not exist; real file is data/brain_domain_sandboxes_v1.json.
Provenance registry has only one registration (trustfield_loops_phase1); its verify receipts were not re-run this pass.
All 6 staleness receipts cluster on 2026-07-07; weekly CI run history unverified.
No ROI attribution linked to memory anywhere in repo.

categories.product_name_status: IN-USE internally as 'L0 repo graph memory'; 'RepoGraph'/'SourceA Repo Memory' commercial branding is external framing.
categories.buyer: Now: internal agents/operators. Later: agent-platform / dev-tools teams paying a 'token tax' to re-orient agents on every repo read.
categories.pain_solved: Agents blindly re-read repos to orient (the design doc cites a ~371k-token blind pass), driving cost, latency, and drift; no compact structural memory or broad-read discipline.
categories.comparison_category: Graphify, code search, repo graph tools
categories.noetfield_differentiation: Zero-token structural repo memory (compact subsystem map + query script) plus a broad-read GATE enforced in AGENTS.md, so orientation costs ~2-6k tokens instead of ~371k. Governance-native (the gate is a rule), not just a search index.

domain_strategy.note: Most mature category and the natural first standalone candidate — but only after measured cross-repo savings receipts.

categories.build_status_rationale: The only category verified as live-running: the builder/query/report and verify receipt exist and the gate text is enforced in AGENTS.md in THIS repo. It runs today.
categories.next_build_phase: Prove generality + savings: add AST import/from edges (edges today are regex path-strings, self-admitted 'not a real import graph'); pilot to one second repo (e.g., TrustField) with its own build+green verify receipt; capture a real before/after token-count receipt from an actual session; fix design-doc drift (broken file refs, stale 703-file/17-subsystem figures).
categories.not_now_boundary: No hosted/cloud service, central cross-repo DB, or multi-tenant SKU; no 'measured production token savings' claim (only build-time estimates + one anecdote); no all-repo rollout or commercial branding; no standalone domain.
categories.receipt_required_to_activate: REPOGRAPH_TOKEN_TAX_RECEIPT — a real before/after token-measurement from an actual multi-agent session AND a green build+verify receipt for the tool running in at least one second repo.
honesty_caveats: No measured token-savings receipt yet — the 2-6k vs 371k figures are estimate + anecdote.
Single-repo only; SourceA/ECOSYSTEM_GRAPH.json is a separate dependency graph, so the 'SourceA Repo Memory' half is unproven.
Edges are regex path-strings, not AST; closer to a file map than a dependency graph.
Gate is convention-only (verify checks the instruction text is present, not runtime obedience).
Lives on non-default branch cursor/language-layer-v1; not confirmed merged to main. Live index reports 19 subsystems (the '22' is the candidate-dir list length).

categories.product_name_status: IN-USE internally as 'P0-PGR' (P0 Prompt Governance Runtime / Dispatch Brain Runtime v1.1).
categories.buyer: Now: internal dogfood. Later: teams running many agent prompts/workflows who need governance, ROI, and receipts over dispatched work.
categories.pain_solved: Prompt hubs/libraries store prompts but don't compile, route, critic, ROI-judge, or receipt-verify them; there is no governance or provenance over the work an agent is dispatched to do.
categories.comparison_category: prompt hubs, prompt management tools, agent prompt libraries
categories.noetfield_differentiation: An evidence-based prompt compiler -> router -> critic -> ROI judge -> receipt verifier: orders become governed, lint-clean packets that are gated, phased (shadow -> cloud-only -> autonomous), and receipted, with founder-unlock gates on each rung.

domain_strategy.note: This is the platform spine (the 'runtime' the North Star routes through), not a near-term standalone SKU.

categories.build_status_rationale: R-ladder (per verified receipts): R1 session-driven PASS; R2 cloud-manual PASS — two GitHub-hosted cloud runs (run IDs 28984157746 / 28984159107, both concluded success, event=workflow_dispatch, branch cursor/language-layer-v1 @ 3f61d41, in-cloud jsonschema validation PASS, artifacts present), cron disabled and R3 founder-gated; R3 cron-shadow HOLD; R4 low-risk auto-dispatch not active; R5 ROI-aware autonomy not active. As a COMMERCIAL product it remains built-not-activated: R3+ are founder-gated, the LLM reasoning stages are still not wired (deterministic Python, $0.00 cost), and there is no external consumer/SKU.

Then close Phase-1 exit and progress the canonical registry lane DECLARED -> VERIFIED after a 24h zero-manual green window. Do NOT wire R4/R5 until their founder-unlock receipts exist.
categories.not_now_boundary: No R3 cron/scheduled automation without FOUNDER-UNLOCK-R3-CRON-SHADOW; no R4 auto-dispatch or R5 ROI-autonomy; no real edit/deploy/merge/send — all founder-gated per the ladder + v1.1 contract. No '24/7 / self-running / autonomous / VERIFIED' claims (R2 manual-green != cron-green). Do not describe the critic/ROI-judge as live model reasoning (deterministic scaffolding). No public product claim.
categories.receipt_required_to_activate: R2 cloud PASS is ACHIEVED (P0PGR_R2_CLOUD_MANUAL_PASS_v1-20260709T001540Z.json). Next activation gate = FOUNDER-UNLOCK-R3-CRON-SHADOW + canonical registry lane DECLARED -> VERIFIED after a 24h zero-manual green window. Commercial activation remains a separate, explicit founder decision.
honesty_caveats: R2 cloud-manual PASS receipt is now in canonical (recovered from git 2f2f38b, hash-verified). The 2 cloud cycle receipts (P0PGR-CYCLE-20260709T000048Z / 000050Z) were never committed and are unrecoverable — their per-run evidence is embedded in the R2 PASS receipt's per_run_checks.
Canonical registry lane p0pgr still reads DECLARED / phase0_shadow — not yet flipped to VERIFIED (needs the 24h zero-manual green window).
R3 cron-shadow is HOLD; R4/R5 not active. Manual-green is not cron-green.
LLM reasoning stages still not wired/metered (deterministic Python, $0.00) — critic/ROI-judge are scaffolding, not live model reasoning.
Bootstrap clone ssot-p0pgr-workflow-bootstrap + a cross-clone DRIFT receipt exist; canonical trails the archived clone on P0-PGR work.

categories.product_name_status: PARTIAL — 'forge-governance'/'forge-core-governance' apps exist in SourceA; 'Forge Factory / Factory Lines' as a named product is framing.
categories.buyer: Now: internal. Later: teams wanting governed cloud execution (vs Fly/Railway/Modal/GHA) where every run carries a gate, receipt, cost token, and ROI control — not raw compute.
categories.pain_solved: Generic runners execute anything with no authority, receipt, ROI, or gate; there is no governance over what ran, its cost, or its outcome.
categories.comparison_category: Fly.io, Railway, Modal, GitHub Actions, agent runners
categories.noetfield_differentiation: A governance OVERLAY on execution: promotion gate + cost tokens + autorun workflows + advisory worker, so each line's runs are gated, costed, and receipted. Not its own compute plane — governed lines on top of GitHub Actions / workers.

domain_strategy.note: Umbrella under sourcea.app first; standalone 'Forge Factory' only after per-line governed-run receipts.

categories.product_name_status: PROPOSED — 'workcell' / 'Forge Workcells' / 'Noetfield Sandboxes' appear NOWHERE in code; in-code framing is 'Cloud Factory Substrate' + 'Sandbox Manager'.
categories.buyer: Now: internal agent runs. Later: teams needing isolated, safe agent execution with branch/PR/preview/rollback (vs Replit/Cursor background agents/Devin).
categories.pain_solved: Agent sandboxes run code without governed isolation guarantees, receipts, or provable rollback; unsafe autonomous execution against real repos.
categories.comparison_category: cloud IDEs, Replit agents, Cursor background agents, Devin-style sandboxes
categories.noetfield_differentiation: Isolated workcells with branch/PR/preview/rollback plus isolation + wall-gate receipts; safe-by-construction (never auto-merge to venture repos). Governance-first isolation, not just an ephemeral container.

domain_strategy.note: Standalone 'Forge Workcells' only after a real factory-run isolation+rollback receipt; today it is design + git-worktree isolation.

categories.build_status_rationale: Git-worktree isolation, a promotion gate, PR-conflict skill, and a synthetic gate-logic fixture exist, but the core product spec (SANDBOX_MANAGER_SPEC_v0, sandbox_ledger_v0, walls W1-W9 exit gate) is 'design only, to be built' and lives only on a different branch (cursor/machine-autonomy-loops-v1), not on disk. No real factory-run receipt; no container/microVM isolation; no preview/rollback mechanism.
categories.next_build_phase: CF-P1 internal founder-only cloud factory: build sandbox_ledger_v0 + allocation (git worktree add per sandbox_id), implement machine-readable walls W1-W9 with a mechanical exit-gate script, and produce ONE real (non-synthetic) factory-run receipt from a live worktree-isolated agent run. Reconcile the L0-graph/doc drift (missing cloud-factory files).
categories.not_now_boundary: No container/microVM isolation claim (only git-worktree exists); no cron/scheduled sandbox automation (needs FOUNDER-UNLOCK-CF-CRON); no preview/rollback product surface; no authenticated client workspaces or paid platform (CF-P2/CF-P5); no auto-merge to venture repos. The existing founder unlock is design-only — it does not authorize building/deploying.
categories.receipt_required_to_activate: WORKCELL_FACTORY_RUN_RECEIPT — a real factory-run receipt at receipts/factory/<FR-ID>/ from a live git-worktree-isolated agent run that allocated a ledger row, hit a wall (BLOCKED_WITH_REASON), and exited with a branch/PR (no auto-merge) — explicitly NOT a step9-style synthetic fixture. Plus a founder build-unlock receipt.
honesty_caveats: Core spec (cloud-factory/*) exists only on branch cursor/machine-autonomy-loops-v1 (commit b4e4cdd), not on checked-out HEAD or disk.
step9 candidate PASS/FAIL/TAMPERED receipts are synthetic fixtures (placeholder SHA256).
Isolation is git-worktree-level only; no container/microVM found.
'preview/rollback' is unevidenced (a planned CF-P4 phase).
brain_domain_sandboxes are brain deploy lanes, not compute/agent-execution sandboxes; a stale prunable worktree exists at /private/tmp/sg-cost-locks-fix.

categories.product_name: Noetfield Workflow Builder / SourceA Agentic Workflows
categories.product_name_status: PROPOSED — no builder product exists; internal 'workflow' means Noetfield's own automation motors (WORKFLOW_CENSUS).
categories.buyer: Now: internal governance. Later: ops teams building AI workflows (vs Zapier/Make/n8n/LangGraph/CrewAI) who need approvals + receipts + cost control + regulated-action gates.
categories.pain_solved: Workflow tools automate steps but have no approval gates, receipts, cost control, or regulated-action boundaries — unsafe for governed/regulated work.
categories.noetfield_differentiation: Workflows with human approvals, receipts, cost/value classification, and regulated-action gates built in; the same P0-PGR dispatch governance applied to multi-step work. Governance-native, not a visual node toy.

domain_strategy.note: Umbrella now; subdomain/standalone only after an externally-authored, receipted workflow exists.

categories.build_status_rationale: A workflow census, its migration, and the P0-PGR dispatch engine exist and run in shadow, but there is no customer-facing builder (no UI, node editor, templates, or user-authored workflows); every 'workflow' is Noetfield's own internal motor, dispatch is shadow-only, and the census's own audit_status is RED (META cost > GUARD+REVENUE). Scaffold, not a builder.
categories.next_build_phase: Build the thinnest external surface that is THIS category's OWN evidence: let ONE design-partner author a workflow packet and watch it run with an approval gate + a receipt + cost + value_class returned — 'define a governed workflow, get its receipt'. NOTE (founder patch): P0-PGR's R2 cloud PASS (CAT-03) is runtime proof for the prompt runtime, NOT proof of a workflow BUILDER — it does not advance this category. No cron/auto-dispatch/ROI-autonomy until their founder-unlock receipts exist.
categories.not_now_boundary: No visual node/drag-drop builder, template marketplace, or multi-tenant SaaS; no Zapier/Make/n8n parity claim; no cron or real dispatch/send/deploy/merge (R3+) without a FOUNDER-UNLOCK receipt; no 'live workflow builder' or '24/7 autonomous' claim (honest rung is R1, R2 only PARTIAL, executions shadow-only).
categories.receipt_required_to_activate: WORKFLOW_GOVERNED_APPROVAL_RECEIPT — one externally/partner-authored workflow packet executed with an approval gate + a schema-valid receipt (cost + value_class). Must be DIRECT evidence for the builder category; the P0-PGR R2 cloud PASS does NOT satisfy it.
honesty_caveats: Founder patch: P0-PGR's R2 cloud PASS does NOT advance this category — it stays partial-scaffold until a direct workflow-builder receipt exists.
No UI / node editor / template library / user-authored workflow of any kind.
No external users, pilots, pricing, SKU, or domain.
Census audit_status RED; 8 NONE loops produced no receipt; cost is declared, not billed ($0 metered).
ssot-p0pgr-workflow-bootstrap is a bootstrap clone; a cross-clone DRIFT receipt exists — git isolation not independently verified.
Only one census run read; weekly schedule not confirmed.

categories.product_name: Noetfield App Factory / SourceA App Builder
categories.product_name_status: CONCEPT — no prompt-to-app engine, template library, or generated-app runtime exists anywhere in the workspace.
categories.buyer: Later: internal-tool builders (vs Lovable/Bolt/Replit/v0/Bubble/Softr) who need GOVERNED prompt-to-app for regulated internal tools, portals, dashboards, and workflows.
categories.pain_solved: Prompt-to-app tools ship ungoverned apps; there is no policy/receipt/approval layer for regulated internal tools.
categories.noetfield_differentiation: Prompt-to-app for GOVERNED internal tools/portals/dashboards/workflows: every generated app carries an approval gate + generation receipt + policy layer. (Aspirational — nothing built.)

domain_strategy.note: Weakest/most immature; may not warrant a distinct product — decide whether it folds into the Studio IDE (CAT-08) before any build.

SourceA/apps/README.md + SourceA/apps/newmatch/README.md (domain-specific scaffolds, not an app builder)

categories.build_status_rationale: No builder engine, no generated-app runtime, and 0 files for 'app builder' in the L0 graph. buildmatch is off-category; noetfield-studio-ide is a code IDE, not a no-code builder. Concept-only.
categories.next_build_phase: Scope-decision SPEC first: decide whether 'no-code app builder' is a distinct product or a generation mode of the Studio IDE (which already has the gate/router). If pursued, deliver 1 spec doc + 1 real prompt -> governed internal tool (form-backed dashboard) that emits an approval-gate receipt and a persisted, viewable artifact.
categories.not_now_boundary: No 'App Factory'/'App Builder'/'prompt-to-app' SKU, page, or deck claim; do not present buildmatch or noetfield-studio-ide as evidence of this category; keep it out of OFFERINGS_LOCKED. Hard hold: no build before a scope-decision spec receipt.
categories.receipt_required_to_activate: APP_FACTORY_SPEC_LOCK_RECEIPT — a locked scope-decision spec PLUS one real execution where a natural-language prompt produces a governed internal tool that is (a) deployed/viewable at a URL, (b) approval-gated, and (c) backed by a persisted generation receipt (prompt -> spec -> app -> gate PASS -> artifact).
honesty_caveats: No prompt-to-app / no-code artifact of any kind in the workspace.
May be miscategorized — candidate to fold into CAT-08 Studio IDE.
buildmatch is a construction action-pack templater (client-side localStorage, no backend).
Not in OFFERINGS_LOCKED (only Trust Brief, Copilot Pack, Bank Pilot are locked SKUs).
Buyer/pain/differentiation are inferred from the category definition — no observed customer or usage.

categories.product_name: Noetfield Studio IDE / Forge Studio / Forge Terminal
categories.product_name_status: REAL app exists as 'noetfield-studio-ide' (v0.1.0 / Phase 0, 'cursor-lite' loop).
categories.buyer: Sina + clients operating the factory — one cockpit for orders, prompts, sandboxes, approvals, runs, receipts, and deploys.
categories.pain_solved: Cursor/VS Code/Retool are generic; nothing ties orders -> prompts -> sandboxes -> approvals -> runs -> receipts -> deploys into one governed cockpit view.
categories.comparison_category: Cursor, VS Code, Retool/admin consoles, internal developer portals
categories.noetfield_differentiation: A governance-native cockpit that surfaces the full order->receipt->deploy chain with worktrees, gate/promote receipts, and an approval flow — the operator surface of the Cloud Factory, not a generic editor.

domain_strategy.note: Local-first single-founder now; a studio.* subdomain once the autonomous loop is green and the app is packaged; standalone-later possible.

categories.build_status_rationale: A real Next.js cockpit runs (via next dev) with gate/promote receipts, live worktrees, a model probe, and a compiled VS Code bridge. But the flagship autonomous loop is NOT green (the cursor-lite sample records verify.ok=false, 'synthesized from partial run'), there is no hosting/auth/multi-tenant, and receipts are dev smoke runs (4 total). Built app, loop + product not activated.
categories.next_build_phase: Green the cursor-lite end-to-end loop: fix the sandbox typecheck so a real run reaches verify.ok=true and emits a genuine gate->verify-PASS->promote receipt. Then package the notarized macOS .app/DMG and finish the Docker command-sandbox isolation lane. Only then add auth/multi-workspace for any hosted offering.
categories.not_now_boundary: No hosted/multi-tenant SaaS; no auth/billing/cloud deployment yet; no 'replaces Cursor/VS Code' claim; keep local-first single-founder until the loop is proven green and packaged. Do not conflate with the adjacent SourceA forge-governance Cursor plugin listing or the SG desktop-app auditor bundles (separate products).
categories.receipt_required_to_activate: STUDIO_LOOP_GREEN_RECEIPT — a real, non-synthesized end-to-end cursor-lite receipt where sandbox -> gate-approve -> verify (typecheck/build) PASS -> promote all succeed on a fresh change (a promote receipt whose linked gate carries verify.ok=true), PLUS a run receipt from an installed/notarized app (not `next dev`).
honesty_caveats: Flagship autonomous loop is not green (verify.ok=false sample).
No deployment/hosting/auth/multi-tenant; runs only via next dev on 127.0.0.1:3005; v0.1.0.
Only 4 receipts, all dev smoke runs (2026-07-04).
vscode-extension compiled but not packaged (.vsix) or published; model probe is multi-model single-provider (Google), not multi-vendor.
Did not boot the app live this pass; runnability inferred from build dir + last-run + receipts.

categories.product_name_status: PROPOSED — 'ReceiptGraph'/'Noetfield Trust Layer' appear NOWHERE in the repo; the underlying receipts/ledger/auditor plumbing is real.
categories.buyer: Every buyer of the other categories + auditors/compliance (vs audit logs/LangSmith/observability/SOC2 evidence tools).
categories.pain_solved: Traces/logs show WHAT happened but not authority, cost, changed files, deploy state, and outcome as verifiable receipts you can independently check.
categories.comparison_category: audit logs, LangSmith traces, observability, SOC2 evidence tools
categories.noetfield_differentiation: Every AI action emits a receipt carrying proof + authority + cost + changed-files + deploy-state + outcome, independently verifiable (birth receipt from a separate Cloudflare-Worker verifier). The trust spine under every other category.

domain_strategy.note: The cross-category trust spine; a subdomain 'ReceiptGraph' only after a unified, queryable, self-audited index exists.

categories.build_status_rationale: Thousands of real receipts, a ledger, an auditor script, an auditor skill, a desktop dashboard, and an independent verifier exist. But the auditor has NEVER run to a persisted output, the three receipt stores are disjoint/un-aggregated, there is no unified schema or query/graph surface, and the product name exists nowhere. Real plumbing, not an activated trust product.
categories.next_build_phase: Activate the auditor: run audit_receipt_ledger_v1.py --write-receipt to produce the first receipt-ledger-audit; extend coverage to all three stores; define a unified receipt schema guaranteeing the six dimensions (proof, authority, cost, changed_files, deploy_state, outcome) in every receipt; build a query/graph/aggregation surface so 'ReceiptGraph' is an actual index.
categories.not_now_boundary: No 'finished trust layer/observability product' claim; no SOC2 or audit-log parity claim; no 'every AI action has a receipt' as a shipped guarantee. Under-claim: real internal governance plumbing, not a sellable audit product.
categories.receipt_required_to_activate: RECEIPTGRAPH_SELF_AUDIT_RECEIPT — a first successful audit_receipt_ledger_v1.py --write-receipt run across a UNIFIED corpus (all three receipt stores) producing an audit receipt with ok=true (or an honest findings list), countersigned by the independent Cloudflare-Worker verifier.
honesty_caveats: Auditor has never been executed (0 receipt-ledger-audit outputs).
Auditor only globs top-level receipts/ non-recursively; misses receipts/p0pgr/ and language_gate/receipts/.
No graph/query/index/unified schema; receipt shapes are heterogeneous per motor.
session-decision-ledger.md self-labels UNVERIFIED and contradicts the later verifier-birth receipt on whether independent verification is real.
Could not re-execute the CF-Worker verifier (birth receipt ~8 days stale); desktop dashboard output is ephemeral /tmp with no launch evidence.

categories.product_name: TrustField / Noetfield AI Value Governance / SourceA / WitnessBC
categories.product_name_status: REAL ventures with in-repo assets for all four lanes; commercial activation differs per lane (see per-lane).
categories.buyer: Per lane: fintech / partner onboarding (TrustField); enterprise AI cost/ROI (Noetfield AI Value Governance); internal control-plane operators (SourceA); proof/media (WitnessBC).
categories.pain_solved: The platform needs external proof that governed execution produces real outcomes in a real vertical — each lane is a proof that the factory works end-to-end with receipts.
categories.comparison_category: vertical proof lanes: fintech onboarding/payment orchestration; enterprise AI ROI; control-plane/factory; proof/media
categories.noetfield_differentiation: Four proof lanes that demonstrate the factory with outcome receipts. TrustField stays a decision/governance layer (never touches value/settlement). Governance + receipts are the throughline vs generic vertical tools.

domain_strategy.note: TrustField = standalone (trustfield.ca); SourceA = standalone (sourcea.app), internal control-plane; Noetfield AI Value Governance = umbrella under noetfield.com; WitnessBC = standalone-later (witnessbc.com, cutover pending).

categories.status_ladder_phase: P3-BUILT-NOT-ACTIVATED (per-lane: TrustField P4/P5 software-live receipt-attested; AIVG P3 pre-launch; SourceA P4 internal/stale; WitnessBC P3 preview-only)
categories.build_status_rationale: All four lanes have real in-repo assets. TrustField is software-live by receipt (LIVE_BPLUS_PASS) but pre-revenue and the entity is only 'reserved to be incorporated'. AI Value Governance is self-labeled pre-launch/pilot. SourceA is a stale internal control-plane, not a sellable SKU. WitnessBC has only a Vercel preview (witnessbc.com cutover pending). NO paying-customer/revenue receipt exists for ANY lane.
categories.next_build_phase: Bring the three non-live lanes to TrustField's evidentiary bar: (1) WitnessBC — execute the witnessbc.com DNS cutover and produce an in-repo live-truth receipt; (2) AI Value Governance — confirm the noetfield.com pages are deployed live AND land the first paying pilot for a delivery receipt; (3) TrustField — sign a paying partner (SOW/invoice); (4) SourceA — refresh PROGRAM_PROGRESS.json and decide internal-control-plane vs productized surface.
categories.not_now_boundary: Do not claim WitnessBC or AI Value Governance are 'live' without an in-repo production live-truth receipt; do not claim any signed customer, revenue, or paid pilot for ANY lane (none found); do not claim TrustField is incorporated or that it holds funds/settles; do not treat SourceA as a sellable external SKU; do not invent a fifth vertical lane.
categories.receipt_required_to_activate: PROOF_LANE_LIVE_TRUTH_RECEIPT (per lane) — a machine live-truth receipt structurally equal to TrustField's partner-access-bplus receipt: HTTP 200 on the production custom domain with expected_build == live_build and pass:true, committed in-repo (witnessbc.com for WitnessBC; the noetfield.com AIVG URLs for Lane 2). For COMMERCIAL activation of any lane, a distinct revenue receipt (signed SOW / paid invoice / Stripe charge); AIVG additionally needs a first customer audit-delivery receipt.
honesty_caveats: No paying-customer/revenue receipt for ANY of the four lanes — all pre-revenue on the evidence seen.
WitnessBC production not confirmed live (Vercel preview_ok only; dns_change_required pending; cutover receipt lives outside the repo).
AI Value Governance self-labels 'pre-launch, pilot phase'; live deploy of noetfield.com pages not confirmed this pass.
TrustField entity only 'reserved to be incorporated' (interim contracting via Noetfield Systems Inc.); live status is receipt-attested, not re-observed; local HEAD ahead of origin.
SourceA PROGRAM_PROGRESS.json is stale (updated 2026-06-19); describes an internal factory, not a sellable SKU.
